input.headline: This is the world’s coolest street. Here’s why

raw.headline: This is the world’s coolest street. Here’s why

winner: It's a street where every crosswalk has a built-in cooler for your soda—because nothing says "cool" like cold drinks and pedestrian hypothermia.

candidates.text: Why did the ice cream truck driver get a parking ticket on the "world's coolest street"? Because his business was *melting* under all that attention.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'melting' are clever and tie into the ice cream theme, with a nice surprise twist related to the street's popularity."}

candidates.text: This street is so cool, I once saw a man attempt to sell me an ice cube. He charged extra for the shipping.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific scenario with a clever twist on 'cool'"}

candidates.text: Why did they name it the "coolest" street? So people would stop asking for directions to the freezer aisle!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the connection between 'coolest street' and 'freezer aisle' is a bit weak and relies on a stretch of logic."}

candidates.text: Why did the street break up with its sidewalk? It needed some *space*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and uses wordplay on 'space,' but it's a bit generic and doesn't strongly tie to the headline about the world’s coolest street."}

candidates.text: It's a street where every crosswalk has a built-in cooler for your soda—because nothing says "cool" like cold drinks and pedestrian hypothermia.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the mention of 'pedestrian hypothermia' feels forced and slightly off-putting, reducing its overall humor impact."}
